body:
Ok, ran another test. This time set my starship config (~/.config/starship.toml) to be the default format variable with everything *except* pijul_channel, and it's still misbehaving, so it seems like its not a pijul channel issue.

```toml
format = """
$username\
$hostname\
$localip\
$shlvl\
$singularity\
$kubernetes\
$directory\
$vcsh\
$fossil_branch\
$fossil_metrics\
$git_branch\
$git_commit\
$git_state\
$git_metrics\
$git_status\
$hg_branch\
$docker_context\
$package\
$c\
$cmake\
$cobol\
$daml\
$dart\
$deno\
$dotnet\
$elixir\
$elm\
$erlang\
$fennel\
$gleam\
$golang\
$guix_shell\
$haskell\
$haxe\
$helm\
$java\
$julia\
$kotlin\
$gradle\
$lua\
$nim\
$nodejs\
$ocaml\
$opa\
$perl\
$php\
$pulumi\
$purescript\
$python\
$quarto\
$raku\
$rlang\
$red\
$ruby\
$rust\
$scala\
$solidity\
$swift\
$terraform\
$typst\
$vlang\
$vagrant\
$zig\
$buf\
$nix_shell\
$conda\
$meson\
$spack\
$memory_usage\
$aws\
$gcloud\
$openstack\
$azure\
$nats\
$direnv\
$env_var\
$mise\
$crystal\
$custom\
$sudo\
$cmd_duration\
$line_break\
$jobs\
$battery\
$time\
$status\
$os\
$container\
$netns\
$shell\
$character"""
```

```bash
bash-5.2$ starship timings

 Here are the timings of modules in your prompt (>=1ms or output):
 buf         -  33562ms  -   ""
 netns       -      1ms  -   ""
 character   -     <1ms  -   "❯ "
 directory   -     <1ms  -   "~ "
 line_break  -     <1ms  -   "\n"
```

Removing buf as well leads to satisfactory results though.

```
bash-5.2$ starship timings

 Here are the timings of modules in your prompt (>=1ms or output):
 c           -   2ms  -   ""
 netns       -   1ms  -   ""
 character   -  <1ms  -   "❯ "
 directory   -  <1ms  -   "~ "
 line_break  -  <1ms  -   "\n"
bash-5.2$ starship timings

 Here are the timings of modules in your prompt (>=1ms or output):
 netns       -   1ms  -   ""
 character   -  <1ms  -   "❯ "
 directory   -  <1ms  -   "~ "
 line_break  -  <1ms  -   "\n"
bash-5.2$ starship timings

 Here are the timings of modules in your prompt (>=1ms or output):
 character   -  <1ms  -   "❯ "
 directory   -  <1ms  -   "~ "
 line_break  -  <1ms  -   "\n"
bash-5.2$ starship timings

 Here are the timings of modules in your prompt (>=1ms or output):
 netns       -   4ms  -   ""
 c           -   1ms  -   ""
 character   -  <1ms  -   "❯ "
 directory   -  <1ms  -   "~ "
 line_break  -  <1ms  -   "\n"
bash-5.2$ starship timings

 Here are the timings of modules in your prompt (>=1ms or output):
 character   -  <1ms  -   "❯ "
 directory   -  <1ms  -   "~ "
 line_break  -  <1ms  -   "\n"
```

Ok, looks like the issue was caused by this drive not being plugged in:

```ini
[Unit]
Description=Mount for drive (1st partition of external drive)

[Mount]
What=LABEL=general-drive
Where=/var/home/craftidore/drive
Type=ext4

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

```ini
[Unit]
Description=Automount general-drive

[Automount]
Where=/var/home/craftidore/drive

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

This made even running `ls` in my home dir hang.
Why is beyond me.

I'm going to leave the issue open anyway, because I still consider the fact that starship wasn't timing out those modules after over 30 seconds to be incorrect behavior. If this is correct, intended behavior then feel free to close.
